content.content:
# Erdős Problem #664

**Problem Number:** 664

**Status:** DISPROVED (solved in the negative)

**Problem Statement:**
Let c<1 be a constant and A₁,…,Aₘ⊆{1,…,n} such that |Aᵢ|>c√n for all i and |Aᵢ∩Aⱼ|≤1 for all i≠j. Must there exist a set B such that B∩Aᵢ≠∅ and |B∩Aᵢ|≪_c 1 for all i?

**Context:**
The problem relates to finite geometry, specifically whether blocking sets must intersect every line in O(1) points.

**Variant:**
Erdős posed in [Er81] an alternative version replacing the pairwise intersection condition with a requirement that every two points appear in exactly one Aᵢ (pairwise balanced block design).

**Resolution:**
Noga Alon disproved the conjecture using projective plane constructions. For large prime powers q with n=m=q²+q+1, he constructed sets where |Aᵢ|≥(2/5)√n with pairwise intersections ≤1, yet any blocking set B meets some Aⱼ in Ω(log n) points.

**Remaining Question:**
The weaker version from [Er81] remains open, though Alon conjectures it's also false.

**Related Problem:** #1159 (weaker version for projective plane lines)

**Tags:** combinatorics

**Sources:** [Er81], [Er97f]

content.text:
Here is the content from [Erdos Problem #664](https://www.erdosproblems.com/664):

---

**Problem 664** -- Status: **Disproved**

**Problem Statement:**
Let c < 1 be a constant and A\_1, ..., A\_m be subsets of {1, ..., n} such that |A\_i| > c*sqrt(n) for all i and |A\_i intersect A\_j| <= 1 for all i != j. Must there exist a set B such that B intersect A\_i is nonempty and |B intersect A\_i| is bounded by a constant depending only on c, for all i?

**Context:** The problem relates to finite geometry -- specifically whether blocking sets must intersect every line in O(1) points.

**Variant:** Erdos posed in [Er81] an alternative version replacing the pairwise intersection condition with a requirement that every two points appear in exactly one A\_i (i.e., a pairwise balanced block design).

**Resolution:** Noga Alon disproved the conjecture using projective plane constructions. For large prime powers q with n = m = q^2 + q + 1, he constructed sets where |A\_i| >= (2/5)*sqrt(n) with pairwise intersections at most 1, yet any blocking set B meets some A\_j in Omega(log n) points.

**Remaining Question:** The weaker version from [Er81] remains open, though Alon conjectures it is also false.

**Related Problem:** #1159 (weaker version for projective plane lines)

**Tags:** Combinatorics

**References:** [Er81], [Er97f]
